footer.description: Modern application for <recording>screen recording</recording> and <editing>video editing</editing>. Designed for the web. No installations, no waiting, directly in your browser.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: At VidFlow, we respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal information. This Privacy Policy describes exactly how we collect, handle, store, and protect your data when you use the VidFlow browser extension and associated services. VidFlow is designed to protect user privacy by processing all video and audio content locally on your own machine.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.content: We collect and handle only the minimum necessary data to provide our screen recording and video editing services:
items: Account Information: Your email address, profile name, and profile picture are collected solely if you choose to sign in using third-party OAuth providers (Google or GitHub). This is used strictly to identify your authenticated session.

Media Content: Screen recordings, webcam video feeds, and microphone audio inputs are captured during active recording sessions. These media files are processed entirely locally on your own device and are never transmitted to our servers.
Local Preferences: Editor configurations, background preferences, canvas settings, and crop parameters are saved locally via browser storage to maintain your editing state.
Technical Session Data: Anonymous browser and operating system details, screen resolution parameters, and extension status parameters are processed to ensure compatibility and system stability.

usage.title: How We Use Your Information
usage.content: We use the collected information strictly for the following purposes:
items: To provide, maintain, and support the screen recording and video editing features.
To securely authenticate your user account and prevent unauthorized access to your settings.
To maintain your active editing projects locally across browser refreshes.
To optimize rendering performance, WebAssembly execution, and browser compatibility.
To respond to your support requests or inquiries at our official support email.

processing.title: Local Browser-Based Processing
processing.badge: All video encoding, rendering, cropping, zooming, and audio mixing happens 100% locally inside your browser using high-performance modern web technologies (WebAssembly and FFmpeg.wasm). No video, audio, or captured screen content is ever uploaded, transmitted, or stored on our servers or shared with any third party. Your work is entirely local, private, and secure.

cookies.title: Cookies and Local Storage
cookies.content: We use standard browser cookies and local storage (IndexedDB, chrome.storage.local) to:
items: Keep your user authentication session active.
Persist your active video/image editing projects and imported media assets locally on your device.
Save your custom settings, such as favorite backgrounds, crop ratios, and UI options.
cookies.note: You can configure your browser to reject cookies and clear local storage, but doing so will erase your locally saved VidFlow projects and log you out.

sharing.title: Information Sharing & Disclosure
sharing.content: We do not sell, trade, rent, or share your personal information or media content with any third party under any circumstances. Data is only disclosed in the following extremely limited scenarios:
items: If required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process to protect our users or public safety.
With your explicit, active consent (such as clicking to export and upload a video to your own external third-party accounts).

security.title: Data Security Measures
security.content: We implement robust technical and organizational security measures to protect your information:
items: All network communication, including authentication and API calls, is encrypted in transit using industry-standard HTTPS (SSL/TLS).
Webcam and microphone access are requested strictly via standard browser security permission dialogs and are active only while recording.
We utilize secure, industry-standard authentication through trusted providers (Google and GitHub OAuth via Supabase).
We do not store your media files on any server, completely eliminating the risk of cloud-based data breaches.

rights.title: Your Rights & Data Controls
rights.content: You retain full control over your personal data and media content:
items: You can instantly delete all your locally saved video projects, imported images, and audio tracks by clearing your browser cache or deleting them inside the library panel.
You can request the permanent deletion of your authenticated account and associated email address by emailing us at alvesoscar517@gmail.com.
You can revoke webcam or microphone permissions at any time through your browser's site settings panel.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: VidFlow is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect or maintain personal information from minors.

changes.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or browser security standards. We will notify you of any updates by revising the 'Last updated' date at the top of this page.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or how your data is handled, please contact our support team at alvesoscar517@gmail.com.

license.title: Open Source License & Compliance
license.content: VidFlow is committed to supporting the open-source community. This extension is a modified version based on the open-source OpenVid project and is released as open-source software under the GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPL-3.0). In full compliance with this copyleft license, we provide our complete, corresponding source code publicly. You are free to inspect, modify, and redistribute the code under the same terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Policy
acceptance.content: By using VidFlow, you acknowledge and agree to the terms of this Privacy Policy and our <terms>Terms of Service</terms>.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By installing, accessing, or using the VidFlow browser extension or website, you agree to be legally bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, you must not use or install our platform.

description.title: 2. Description of Service
description.content: VidFlow is a local-first browser-based video editing and screen recording application that allows you to:
items: Record your browser tabs, specific windows, or entire screen directly from your browser.
Record webcam video overlay (camera bubble) and microphone audio input.
Edit recordings using professional local tools including cinematic zooms, mockup layouts, custom backgrounds, audio syncing, and annotation elements.
Export and render your finalized video projects locally in multiple resolutions.

account.title: 3. User Registration & Accounts
account.content: To access certain personalized features or manage cloud-based configurations, you may choose to log in using trusted third-party OAuth authentication:
items: Google OAuth authentication
GitHub OAuth authentication
account.note: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your authentication sessions and credentials.

acceptable.title: 4. Acceptable Use and Restrictions
acceptable.content: By utilizing VidFlow, you agree to use the service in compliance with all applicable laws and strictly agree NOT to:
items: Upload, record, or process any content that is illegal, defamatory, offensive, or violates the intellectual property rights of any third party.
Attempt to bypass, disable, or interfere with any security-related features, quotas, or authentications of the extension.
Use the platform for any fraudulent or malicious activities, including distributing malware or phishing.
Attempt to reverse engineer, decompile, or extract the proprietary core libraries of the extension without authorization.

intellectual.title: 5. Intellectual Property and Content Ownership
intellectual.content: You retain 100% ownership and intellectual property rights over any videos, audio tracks, images, or assets that you import, record, or edit using VidFlow. We only process your files temporarily and locally in your browser to provide the editing service. We do not store, copy, share, or access your media content.

limitation.title: 6. Disclaimer of Warranties & Limitation of Liability
limitation.content: VidFlow is provided on an 'as is' and 'as available' basis without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. To the maximum extent permitted by law, VidFlow and its developers shall not be liable for:
items: Any loss of files, video recordings, or edited projects stored locally in your browser's cache.
Any temporary service interruptions or performance slowdowns during high CPU-intensity rendering.
Any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages resulting from your use of the extension.
Any device or browser incompatibility issues.

modifications.title: 7. Modifications to Service & Terms
modifications.content: We reserve the right to modify, update, or temporarily suspend the extension's features or services at any time without notice. We may also revise these Terms of Service periodically. Your continued use of VidFlow after updates constitutes your agreement to the new terms.

law.title: 8. Governing Law
law.content: These terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of your local jurisdiction, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

contact.title: 9. Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions, concerns, or feedback regarding these Terms of Service, please contact our support team at alvesoscar517@gmail.com.

acceptance2.content: By using VidFlow, you also accept our <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

hero.title: Create demos and <screen>screen</screen>
hero.description: Add smooth zooms, mockups, customize backgrounds, camera, audio, elements, and export professional demos.
hero.uploadHint: Drag a video or click to select

steps.title: Create your shot in
steps.subtitle: Follow these instructions to capture your screen. The whole process happens in your browser, no installation required.

step1.title: Start capturing
step1.description: Click the button below. Your browser will ask you what you want to share. For optimal performance and cleaner shots, we recommend selecting a <tab>Tab</tab> or <window>Specific window</window>.
step1.mobileAlert: Screen recording is not available on mobile devices. Please use a desktop computer.

step2.title: Hide distractions
step2.description: When sharing your screen, your browser will automatically show a floating bar notifying that the capture is active. Click <hide>Hide</hide> to make sure it doesn't appear in your final video.

step3.title: Get ready for action
step3.description: No rush. Once you approve the permissions in step 1, we will start a <countdown>3-second</countdown> countdown. You will see the indicator in the browser tab so you know exactly when to start speaking or moving the mouse.

visual.title: Switch tabs!
visual.subtitle: The recording will start on the screen you selected.

step4.title: Cut and to the editor
step4.description: When you finish your explanation, simply stop the recording. The file will not be downloaded immediately; it will magically move to our web editor so you can apply zooms, backgrounds, and crops.

countdown.title: Switch tabs now!
countdown.subtitle: Recording will start on the screen you selected.

processing.title: Processing your recording
processing.subtitle: Preparing your video for the editor...

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Choose your preferred method to continue
login.disclaimer: By continuing, you accept our <terms>Terms of Service</terms> and <privacy>Privacy Policy</privacy>.

errors.generic: Error signing in. Please try again.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The route you are looking for does not exist in our editor.

page.title: Support the development
